stem: A scatterplot of monthly electricity cost versus square footage of apartments has a line of best fit $y = 0.12x + 45$, where $x$ is square footage and $y$ is cost in dollars. According to the model, what is the predicted cost for a 900-square-foot apartment?
A line of best fit for a scatterplot relating weeks of training $x$ to miles run per week $y$ is $y = 2x + 6$. What does the value $6$ represent in this context?

choices.text: The increase in miles per week for each additional week of training.
The predicted miles run per week after 0 weeks of training.
The total number of weeks in the study.
The maximum miles a runner can achieve.

explanation: In $y = mx + b$, the $y$-intercept $b$ is the predicted value of $y$ when $x = 0$.

stem: The line of best fit for a scatterplot of plant height $y$ (in cm) versus days since planting $x$ is $y = 0.8x + 3$. According to the model, by how much does the predicted plant height increase for each additional day?

choices.text: $3$ cm

explanation: The slope of the line of best fit gives the predicted change in $y$ per one-unit increase in $x$.

stem: A scatterplot of $y$ versus $x$ has line of best fit $y = -1.5x + 20$. What is the predicted value of $y$ when $x = 4$?
A scatterplot relates the weight of a package $x$ (in pounds) to the shipping cost $y$ (in dollars). The line of best fit is $y = 0.75x + 5$. What does the slope $0.75$ represent?

choices.text: The fixed shipping fee for any package.
The total cost to ship a 1-pound package.
The predicted increase in shipping cost for each additional pound.
The percent of packages that weigh less than 1 pound.

explanation: Slope is the predicted change in $y$ for a one-unit change in $x$ — here, dollars per additional pound.

stem: A line of best fit for a scatterplot of ice cream sales $y$ (in hundreds of dollars) versus daily high temperature $x$ (in $^\circ$F) is $y = 0.35x - 12$. Based on the model, which is the best interpretation of the slope?

choices.text: For each $1^\circ$F increase in temperature, predicted sales increase by about $\$0.35$.
When the temperature is $0^\circ$F, predicted sales are $-\$1{,}200$.
For every $\$100$ in sales, the temperature rises about $0.35^\circ$F.
For each $1^\circ$F increase in temperature, predicted sales increase by about $\$35$.

explanation: Slope $=0.35$ (hundreds of dollars) per degree, i.e. about $\$35$ per $^\circ$F increase.

stem: A biologist collects data on the length $x$ (cm) and mass $y$ (g) of a species of fish. The line of best fit is $y = 18x - 25$. A fish in the data set has length $12$ cm and mass $205$ g. What is the residual for this observation?
explanation: Predicted: $18(12) - 25 = 191$. Residual $=$ observed $-$ predicted $= 205 - 191 = 14$.

stem: A study of 30 used cars produced a line of best fit $y = -1{,}250x + 22{,}000$, where $x$ is the age of the car in years and $y$ is the resale price in dollars. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the slope in this context?

choices.text: A new car is predicted to cost $\$1{,}250$.
The car loses $\$22{,}000$ of value each year.
For each additional $\$1{,}250$ in price, the car is one year older.
For each additional year of age, the predicted resale price decreases by $\$1{,}250$.

explanation: A negative slope of $-1{,}250$ means the predicted $y$ (price) drops by $\$1{,}250$ for each one-unit increase in $x$ (year of age).

stem: A scatterplot of hours studied $x$ and test score $y$ for a class has line of best fit $y = 6.5x + 52$. Two students studied for the same number of hours; one scored $10$ points higher than the line predicts, and the other scored $4$ points lower than the line predicts. What is the difference between the two students' residuals?
explanation: Residuals are $+10$ and $-4$. Their difference is $10 - (-4) = 14$.

stem: The line of best fit for a scatterplot of restaurant seating capacity $x$ and nightly revenue $y$ (in dollars) passes through the points $(40, 1{,}600)$ and $(100, 4{,}000)$. What is the slope of the line of best fit?
explanation: Slope $= \frac{4000 - 1600}{100 - 40} = \frac{2400}{60} = 40$ dollars per seat.

stem: For a data set relating daily average temperature $x$ (in $^\circ$C) to the number of cups of hot chocolate sold $y$, the line of best fit is $y = -3.2x + 96$. Which interpretation of the $y$-intercept is best, assuming $x = 0$ is within the data range?

choices.text: For every cup sold, the temperature drops by $3.2^\circ$C.
The average temperature in the data was $96^\circ$C.
At $96^\circ$C, the model predicts no cups sold.
At $0^\circ$C, the predicted number of cups sold is $96$.

explanation: The $y$-intercept of a regression line gives the predicted $y$-value when $x = 0$.

stem: The line of best fit for a scatterplot of library visits $x$ (in thousands) and book checkouts $y$ (in thousands) per month is $y = 2.4x + 1.2$. According to the model, how many thousand checkouts are predicted for a month with $6$ thousand library visits?

A scatterplot of daily calorie intake $x$ and bodyweight gain $y$ (over 4 weeks) has line of best fit $y = 0.004x - 8$. According to the model, what intake corresponds to a predicted weight gain of $0$?

The line of best fit for a scatterplot of advertising spend $x$ (in thousands of dollars) and weekly revenue $y$ (in thousands of dollars) is $y = 3.6x + 11$. A particular week had $x = 10$ and actual revenue of $y = 52$ thousand dollars. What is the residual for that week, in thousands of dollars?

A scatterplot of age $x$ (in months) and vocabulary size $y$ (in words) for toddlers aged 18 to 36 months has line of best fit $y = 22x - 300$. According to the model, what is the predicted vocabulary size for a 24-month-old?

A scatterplot of hours of sleep $x$ and reaction-time score $y$ (lower is faster) has line of best fit $y = -14x + 420$, valid for $4 \le x \le 10$. Student P slept $5$ hours and scored $370$; Student Q slept $9$ hours and scored $280$. Which statement correctly compares the two residuals?

choices.text: P's residual is positive and Q's is negative, with equal magnitudes.
Both residuals are positive, and P's is larger.
Both residuals are negative, and Q's is larger in magnitude.
P's residual is $20$ and Q's residual is $-14$.

explanation: Predicted P: $-14(5)+420=350$, residual $370-350=+20$. Predicted Q: $-14(9)+420=294$, residual $280-294=-20$. Same magnitude, opposite signs.

stem: Data on 25 cities produced a line of best fit $p = 0.018c + 2.4$, where $c$ is annual coffee sales (in thousands of cups) and $p$ is average annual rainfall (in inches). One city had $c = 150$ and $p = 6.1$. By how much, and in which direction, does the model's prediction differ from the observed rainfall?

choices.text: The model overpredicts by about $1.0$ inch.
The model overpredicts by about $5.1$ inches.
The model underpredicts by about $1.0$ inch.
The model underpredicts by about $5.1$ inches.

explanation: Predicted $p = 0.018(150) + 2.4 = 5.1$. Observed $6.1 > 5.1$, so the model underpredicts by $1.0$ inch.

stem: A line of best fit for a scatterplot passes through the points $(15, 42)$ and $(35, 102)$. Based on this line, what is the predicted value of $y$ when $x = 25$?

A scatterplot of monthly water usage $y$ (in gallons) versus household size $x$ has line of best fit $y = 850x + 1{,}100$. A statistician concludes: 'Each additional household member is associated with about $850$ more gallons of water used per month.' Which statement best evaluates this interpretation within the data range $1 \le x \le 8$?

choices.text: It is incorrect because correlation does not imply causation.
It is incorrect because the intercept $1{,}100$ should be used instead.
It is correct: the slope of a line of best fit gives the predicted change in $y$ per one-unit change in $x$.
It is incorrect because a line of best fit cannot be used to describe group differences.

explanation: The slope of a line of best fit quantifies the predicted change in $y$ for each one-unit increase in $x$; the interpretation restates this correctly.

stem: The line of best fit for a scatterplot of $x$ and $y$ is $y = 4x - 3$. When the $x$-value of each data point is increased by $2$ (and $y$-values unchanged), what is the equation of the new line of best fit?
explanation: Replacing $x$ with $x' = x + 2$ means $x = x' - 2$, so $y = 4(x' - 2) - 3 = 4x' - 11$. The slope is unchanged; the intercept shifts.
